description: M2 Providers — AI provider registry, API key encryption, Ollama support, model selector, per-project preferences

userStories.title: Create providers database table and schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a table to store AI provider configurations and encrypted API keys.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/db/schema/providers.ts with id, workspaceId, name ('anthropic'|'openai'|'google'|'openrouter'|'ollama'|'custom'), displayName, baseUrl, apiKeyEncrypted (text), isActive (boolean), createdAt, updatedAt
Add workspace_id FK to workspaces table
Update barrel export in schema/index.ts
Migration generates and runs successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create project-model preferences table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to store per-project model preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/db/schema/project-preferences.ts with id, projectId (FK), providerId (FK), defaultModel, taskTypeOverrides (JSON), createdAt
taskTypeOverrides stores a map of task type to model string, e.g. { 'code': 'claude-sonnet-4', 'review': 'claude-haiku-4-5' }
Update barrel export in schema/index.ts
Migration generates and runs successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement AES-256 encryption utility for API keys
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to encrypt and decrypt API keys at rest so they are never stored in plaintext.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/crypto/keys.ts with encryptApiKey(plaintext) and decryptApiKey(ciphertext) functions
Uses AES-256-GCM via Node.js crypto module
Encryption key derived from INSOMNIAC_SECRET env var (or auto-generated and stored in data/.secret on first run)
Ciphertext includes IV and auth tag for GCM
Round-trip test: encrypt then decrypt returns original value
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: data/.secret is gitignored. If INSOMNIAC_SECRET is set, it takes precedence over the file.

userStories.title: Create provider registry service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service layer that manages provider CRUD and exposes available models.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/providers/registry.ts with ProviderRegistry class
Methods: listProviders(), getProvider(id), addProvider(config), updateProvider(id, config), removeProvider(id)
addProvider encrypts the API key before storing
getProvider decrypts the API key only when needed for API calls (never returned to frontend)
listProviders returns providers WITHOUT decrypted keys
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add built-in model definitions for each provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a static registry of known models per provider for the model selector.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/providers/models.ts with PROVIDER_MODELS constant
Anthropic: claude-sonnet-4, claude-haiku-4-5
OpenAI: gpt-4o, o3
Google: gemini-2.0-flash, gemini-2.5-pro
OpenRouter: custom model string input
Ollama: dynamic (fetched from /api/tags endpoint)
Each model entry has: id, name, displayName, provider, contextWindow, costPerInputToken, costPerOutputToken
Export getModelsForProvider(providerName) function
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Ollama provider with dynamic model discovery
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Ollama as a first-class provider that discovers available models automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/providers/ollama.ts with OllamaProvider class
fetchModels() queries GET {baseUrl}/api/tags and returns parsed model list
Default baseUrl is http://localhost:11434
No API key required — Ollama is local-only
Connection test method: ping() verifies Ollama is reachable
Returns models in the same format as static model definitions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create providers REST API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CRUD endpoints for managing AI providers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/routes/providers.ts as a Fastify plugin
GET /api/providers returns all providers (without decrypted keys)
POST /api/providers adds a new provider with encrypted API key
PUT /api/providers/:id updates provider config
DELETE /api/providers/:id removes a provider
GET /api/providers/:id/models returns available models for a provider
GET /api/providers/ollama/models fetches live model list from Ollama
Input validation on all POST/PUT bodies (API key never returned in responses)
Register routes in server/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create project preferences REST API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need endpoints to manage per-project model preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: Add routes to server/routes/providers.ts or create server/routes/preferences.ts
GET /api/projects/:id/preferences returns the project's model preferences
PUT /api/projects/:id/preferences updates the project's default provider, model, and task type overrides
If no preferences exist, creates them on first PUT
Input validation on PUT body
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create provider setup API hooks for frontend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TanStack Query hooks to manage providers from the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/api/providers.ts with useProviders(), useAddProvider(), useUpdateProvider(), useDeleteProvider() hooks
Create useProviderModels(providerId) hook for fetching available models
All mutations invalidate the 'providers' query key on success
Provider type interface does NOT include apiKey field (security)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Settings view with Providers tab
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Settings view where I can add and manage AI providers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/views/SettingsView.tsx with a tabbed layout
First tab: 'Providers' — lists all configured providers
Each provider card shows: name, status (active/inactive), number of available models
Add 'settings' to MainView routing (accessible via toolbar Settings button)
Add 'settings' to the layout store's MainView type
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add provider configuration form
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add a new AI provider by entering its name, API key, and optional base URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an 'Add Provider' button to the Providers tab in SettingsView
Clicking opens a form with: provider type dropdown (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, OpenRouter, Ollama, Custom), API key input (password field), optional base URL input
For Ollama: hide API key field, show base URL with default http://localhost:11434
For Custom: show both API key and base URL fields
Submit calls useAddProvider() mutation
Form closes and provider list refreshes on success
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create model selector dropdown component
userStories.description: As a user, I want a reusable model selector dropdown that shows available models grouped by provider.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/ui/ModelSelector.tsx
Dropdown shows models grouped by provider name
Each option shows model display name and provider
For Ollama provider, models are fetched dynamically
Component accepts value, onChange, and optional className props
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Integrate model selector into pipeline stage creation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose a model when adding a stage to a pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: When adding a stage via the pipeline API, the model field uses the ModelSelector component
Default model comes from the project's preferences (if set) or the first available model
Model selection is stored in the pipeline_stages.model column
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Seed default providers on first run
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the app to create placeholder provider entries on first run so users see the provider list.
acceptanceCriteria: On server startup, if providers table is empty, create entries for Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, OpenRouter, and Ollama
All seeded providers have isActive=false (no API key configured yet)
Ollama seeded with default baseUrl http://localhost:11434
Seed logic runs in the providers route plugin registration
Typecheck passes
